title: Athens 7-Day Heritage Tour
metaTitle: Athens 7-Day Heritage Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 7-day trip to Athens, Greece: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Acropolis & Parthenon and Acropolis Museum. Prices from $1528 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 7 days discovering Athens, Greece — Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 33°C in July-August) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 13-14°C); the archaeological sites offer little shade, so midday summer heat is intense. April to June and September to October offer warm, sunny days without peak-summer heat or crowds. This itinerary blends Acropolis & Parthenon, Acropolis Museum, Plaka with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
6 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Acropolis & Parthenon, Acropolis Museum, Plaka
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Athens
itinerary.description: Land at Athens International Airport (ATH) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Acropolis & Parthenon — Iconic ancient citadel crowned by the Parthenon temple. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Athens: Acropolis Museum & Ancient Agora
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Acropolis Museum — Modern museum built over ruins, displaying Acropolis artifacts. In the afternoon, continue to Ancient Agora — Athens' ancient marketplace and the well-preserved Temple of Hephaestus. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Athens: Panathenaic Stadium & Temple of Olympian Zeus
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Panathenaic Stadium — All-marble stadium of the first modern Olympics (1896). In the afternoon, continue to Temple of Olympian Zeus — Colossal columns of once the largest temple in Greece. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Cape Sounion & Temple of Poseidon
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Cape Sounion & Temple of Poseidon, a popular side trip from Athens. Return to Athens in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Cape Sounion & Temple of Poseidon

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Kerameikos ancient cemetery — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Athens: Roman Agora & National Archaeological Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Roman Agora — Roman-era marketplace with the Tower of the Winds. In the afternoon, continue to National Archaeological Museum — Greece's largest collection of antiquities. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Byzantine and Christian Museum or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to ATH for your departure flight.

travelTips: Buy the Acropolis combined ticket (covers 7 sites) online for timed entry.
Visit the Acropolis right at opening (8am) to beat both heat and tour-bus crowds.
Major archaeological sites are free on the first Sunday of the month, November-March.
Metro Line 3 runs directly to/from the airport, avoiding taxi surcharges.
Book Cape Sounion sunset trips a day ahead in peak summer.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Sturdy walking shoes for uneven ancient stone paths
A sun hat and high-SPF sunscreen for shade-free archaeological sites
Modest clothing for church and monastery visits
Light breathable clothing for hot summer months
A light jacket for cooler winter evenings
thingsToAvoid: Taxi drivers who claim the meter is broken — insist on it or agree a fixed price upfront.
Restaurant touts pulling tourists in around Plaka's main strip.
Walking alone late at night around Omonia Square.
Climbing on ancient ruins for photos — fines apply.
Visiting the Acropolis at midday in summer heat without water and sun protection.
topRestaurants: Karamanlidika tou Fani — Greek Deli & Meze ($$)
instagramSpots: Acropolis at sunset from Areopagus Hill
Plaka rooftop tavernas facing the Parthenon
adventureActivities: Rock climbing near Varympompi outside Athens
Sailing day trips to the Saronic Islands
luxuryExperiences: Private after-hours tour of the Acropolis
Yacht day trip to Hydra, Poros or Aegina
Rooftop suite stay with Acropolis views
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dinner in Plaka facing the Parthenon
Private sunset tour of Cape Sounion
Couples day cruise to the Saronic Islands
budgetExperiences: Free wander through Plaka and Anafiotika
Climbing Lycabettus or Philopappos for free Acropolis views

festivalCalendar.name: Athens Epidaurus Festival
festivalCalendar.timing: June to August, ancient theatre and music performances

festivalCalendar.name: Greek Orthodox Easter
festivalCalendar.timing: April (moveable), the most important religious holiday

localEtiquette: Cover shoulders and knees when entering churches and monasteries.
Never make the "moutza" open-palm gesture — it is a deeply offensive insult in Greece.
Meals are long, unhurried affairs; waitstaff won't rush you and won't bring the bill until asked.
A polite "Yassas" (hello) when entering small shops or tavernas is appreciated.
Bargaining is not expected in shops but is acceptable at flea markets like Monastiraki.
localTransportGuide: The Athens Metro is modern, cheap and connects the airport, Piraeus port and major sights like the Acropolis and Syntagma directly; a single ticket covers metro, bus, tram and trolley for 90 minutes. Central Athens is walkable but hilly and cobblestoned near the Acropolis, so comfortable shoes matter more than taxis for sightseeing.
bestTimeToVisit: April to June and September to October offer warm, sunny days without peak-summer heat or crowds.
weather: Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 33°C in July-August) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 13-14°C); the archaeological sites offer little shade, so midday summer heat is intense.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Athens?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Athens?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to October offer warm, sunny days without peak-summer heat or crowds. Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 33°C in July-August) and mild, wetter winters (highs around 13-14°C); the archaeological sites offer little shade, so midday summer heat is intense.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Athens safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Athens is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Athens?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Ava Costa
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Benaki Museum alone was worth the trip, but combined with National Archaeological Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Fatima Ivanov
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Panathenaic Stadium alone was worth the trip, but combined with Cape Sounion Temple of Poseidon this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Santos
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Acropolis Museum alone was worth the trip, but combined with Mount Lycabettus this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Ethan Santos
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Monastiraki Flea Market and Acropolis & Parthenon. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Sofia Wang
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. National Garden in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Marco Novak
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Changing of the Evzones Guard and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Marco Kowalski
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Areopagus Hill and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Nadia Johnson
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Athens is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Philopappos Hill. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
